[2018-04-08 15:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Could someone please point out the biggest mistakes that I made? yttcc-KTUg0
[2018-04-08 15:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Was Chieftain hold a bad way to deal with freeze threat?
[2018-04-08 15:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Also if I floated green I may have been able to get a Tesla along the initial Chieftain
[2018-04-08 15:34] tophwells (Toph): You played quite reactively in the early game. You got every attacker after your opponent, and every defender before them.
[2018-04-08 15:36] tophwells (Toph): I'm not sure if it was right to buy Chieftain at all after your opponent got Endotherm kit - lifespan blockers are a liability against freeze. Once you did have it, blocking with it was probably right though.
[2018-04-08 15:38] p0lari (Polari): the chieftain was fine
[2018-04-08 15:38] p0lari (Polari): even if you don't attack with it it's efficient soak, which is exactly what he needed
[2018-04-08 15:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So was I supposed to try and get first Endo?
[2018-04-08 15:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I felt that cuts too much no?
[2018-04-08 15:38] p0lari (Polari): if the opponent wants to blow their kit on chilling it from 3 lifespan you're totally fine with that
[2018-04-08 15:39] p0lari (Polari): yeah idk, I don't think there's any obvious thing you could do differently
[2018-04-08 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Another issue I came across was when I ran out of Matrix supply
[2018-04-08 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): If I lose Matrix on Matrix, he can start freezing it with Bite+2 Cryo charge
[2018-04-08 15:39] p0lari (Polari): going third engi into conduit first p2 just ends up half a turn ahead
[2018-04-08 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Is that fine to allow?
[2018-04-08 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess it is fine otherwise I overdefend for 5 just to deny an ok trade
[2018-04-08 15:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well I felt if I don't go 3rd engi I just lose to p2's natural conduit
[2018-04-08 15:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): At least in the 3rd engi the reactive Conduit from p1 is better than slow conduit
[2018-04-08 15:41] p0lari (Polari): I mean, I'm just saying if you both play the obvious line it's better for p2, usually there's some unintuitive better line there for the disadvantaged player but I sure am not seeing it at a casual glance
[2018-04-08 15:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But ok you're saying maybe no Chieftain?
[2018-04-08 15:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But then I have to make a lot of forcefields to deal with the freeze threat no?
[2018-04-08 15:41] p0lari (Polari): and that imo the reason you lost was that the p2 obvious line was half a turn faster and not because of any specific blunders
[2018-04-08 15:42] p0lari (Polari): no I was saying the chieftain was fine

[2018-04-08 15:43] p0lari (Polari): I mean, I'm not 100% sold? the first one was fairly awkward since you weren't getting hit for 11 yet but it worked out
[2018-04-08 15:43] p0lari (Polari): you got 6 soak out of it
[2018-04-08 15:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Should I have been losing Chiefs on defense from the start?
[2018-04-08 15:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I was thinking maybe it was better to keep alive on 3 lifespan cos I still didn't have enough Rhinos to completely cover the freeze threat
[2018-04-08 15:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And then lose it on 2 Lifespan
[2018-04-08 15:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): (Keep it on 3ls to rethreaten 2dmg)

[2018-04-09 04:37] damian.2137 (D4mian): could anyone tell me if there's a way of punishing this that doesn't result in an instaloss to the huge eco boost of the AI?
[2018-04-09 04:37] damian.2137 (D4mian): that early double dynamo looks like something that can be exploited
[2018-04-09 04:39] damian.2137 (D4mian): I was trying to go conduit+blastforge into gauss+steelsplitter, but I got steamrolled later because of the dynamos
[2018-04-09 04:39] .bky_1556 (bky): I don't think there is here; you should have opened DD/1D to try to keep up.
[2018-04-09 04:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): why did u not do dd/d1
[2018-04-09 04:40] Deleted User (Deleted User): auric is a non factor so i guess we default to throium being 1 i guess
[2018-04-09 04:41] .bky_1556 (bky): You're still in a position where you can double Thorium yourself and be only a little behind.
[2018-04-09 04:41] damian.2137 (D4mian): good to know that i was trying to win an unwinnable game by reverting to that point :Lul:
[2018-04-09 04:41] damian.2137 (D4mian): you saved me a few hours
[2018-04-09 04:41] .bky_1556 (bky): It's not exactly unwinnable.
[2018-04-09 04:42] .bky_1556 (bky): You are behind, but not by an obviously-game-losing margin. Remember that the AI needs to cut a drone next turn.
[2018-04-09 04:43] Deleted User (Deleted User): it's not really "cutting" the drone as he got it and more when he got the throiums
[2018-04-09 04:45] .bky_1556 (bky): I think DC2 is playable. You are threatening extra venge cannons by having fewer thoriums 😉
[2018-04-09 04:46] .bky_1556 (bky): This isn't a particularly good breachproof set because of Xeno-Xaetron.

[2018-04-09 04:49] potatorice (Potatoes): when is thorium even not playable?
[2018-04-09 04:51] .bky_1556 (bky): When P1 can breach during  the downturn when P2 has no tech and no income.
[2018-04-09 04:51] .bky_1556 (bky): When 14 drone-income is too slow.

[2018-04-09 04:54] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): When can Savior be viable?
[2018-04-09 04:57] .bky_1556 (bky): Good defense. No punish for P1 3rd engineer.
[2018-04-09 04:58] .bky_1556 (bky): and no Big Red timing before turn 9.

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-04-09) ---
[2018-04-09 19:07] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Assuming no exploit freezes, are there any cases where you wouldn't freeze a 2ls Grimbotch with Vai?
[2018-04-09 19:10] apooche: You might freeze something bigger to breach more tarsiers.
[2018-04-09 19:10] apooche: But doing less total damage
[2018-04-09 19:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Mm, I was thinking in a case where we don't breach and have no exploit options
[2018-04-09 19:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Freezing Grimbotch twice doesn't lose damage
[2018-04-09 19:11] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Though I guess there might be cases where they would attack on the 1ls turn, cos they just reached a threshold of resources to build a defense they couldn't build the previous turn
[2018-04-09 19:12] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But then if you don't freeze the 2ls botch then they can keep it anyway and attack
[2018-04-09 19:12] apooche: Like sometimes chilling a 2ls botch means you are under extra pressure
[2018-04-09 19:12] apooche: Kill 1 less cybe etc
[2018-04-09 19:13] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Ah depends on the absorber I guess
[2018-04-09 19:13] apooche: Chilling a 2 ls botch with Vai should usually be bad. You just lose a tempo on offense
[2018-04-09 19:13] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): They have to overdefend `absorber-1`
[2018-04-09 19:13] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So with Wall as absorb, freezing the Botch kills everything else assuming they defended for exact+1
[2018-04-09 19:14] apooche: Everything but 1 health
[2018-04-09 19:14] apooche: Botch has less health than Wall and they defended wall chill
[2018-04-09 19:15] apooche: Chilling botch twice though is just paying 1+1 attack for 2
[2018-04-09 19:15] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Oh but if they plan to lose Botch then freezing botch kills 1 more non-botch defense
[2018-04-09 19:16] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So if all their non-botch soak is Cybe then freezing 2ls botch is better
[2018-04-09 19:17] apooche: Sometimes. If they had a 2ls, 3ls, cybe, then chilling 2 ls means the 3ls dies instead, but they keep cybe. Or something like that
[2018-04-09 19:18] darkeisbein: Prismatacoach is a thing again 😮
[2018-04-09 19:18] darkeisbein: I thought we had lost him
[2018-04-09 19:18] .bky_1556 (bky): You could freeze the botch to force them to sacrifice a borehole patroller instead of botch+engineer.
[2018-04-09 19:18] .bky_1556 (bky): Or, less likely, soak with a protoplasm instead of tanking on it.
[2018-04-09 19:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea if they have valuable defense then freezing 2ls into 1ls kills a valuable defender a turn faster right?
[2018-04-09 19:19] .bky_1556 (bky): In the protoplasm case, it might be your only chance to avoid being breached badly yourself.
[2018-04-09 19:20] .bky_1556 (bky): Particularly if it's a 2 plasm defense.
[2018-04-09 19:21] apooche: Freezing botch to kill borehole usually won’t work. If absorber is Wall, you are just giving them the choice between wall and borehole absorb. If the absorber is bigger than Wall, just freeze that instead of botch
[2018-04-09 19:21] .bky_1556 (bky): If the absorber is another borehole?
[2018-04-09 19:22] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Like if they had Cybe+2lsBotch+Wall and we had 4 damage, and they have a 2nd Cybe coming in next turn and we will still have 4 dmg next turn, then we get to kill one of the Cybes a turn faster by freezing the Botch on 2ls. This doesn't result in them having less damage though, so maybe in this case it doesn't matter. It'd matter with Reso forcefields though
[2018-04-09 19:23] apooche: Then yeah, chill botch to breach for exact against borehole. Usually don’t let them do that though as the borehole player I think
[2018-04-09 19:23] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I mean ok this example is bad cos we get 1 free dmg by freezing wall
[2018-04-09 19:23] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But assuming we had 3 more damage and they had another wall or something
[2018-04-09 19:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess if it results in them losing a damage, then we're fine with dealing 1 less damage overall
[2018-04-09 19:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In the Reso+FF example
[2018-04-09 19:25] apooche: Well freezing wall breaches exact still
[2018-04-09 19:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Let's ignore the Cybe case cos in that one it doesn't matter if we freeze the Botch or not
[2018-04-09 19:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Cos Cybes can only fire once
[2018-04-09 19:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But if they had Wall Wall 2lsBotch Forcefield and we had 7 damage
[2018-04-09 19:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well now we get an exploit by freezing botch
[2018-04-09 19:27] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So I guess we need to add an Engi and have 8 damage?
[2018-04-09 19:27] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So Wall Wall 2lsBotch Forcefield(Reso) Engi vs 8 damage
[2018-04-09 19:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): If we freeze wall then sure we kill 2 damage, but we deal 1 less damage overall
[2018-04-09 19:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So assuming they wanna hold botch next turn as well
[2018-04-09 19:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It's better to freeze botch in this case
[2018-04-09 19:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Gotta freeze either Botch or Wall to kill the Forcefield
[2018-04-09 19:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In that case we do have to overdefend 1 though
[2018-04-09 19:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So gotta weigh dealing 1 extra damage to them vs us overdefending for 1
[2018-04-09 19:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Unless we can gambit vs the Botch hold
[2018-04-09 19:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So great, didn't reach any conclusion
[2018-04-09 19:37] .bky_1556 (bky): Try with no walls and with a Colossus tanking in back.

[2018-04-09 19:40] .bky_1556 (bky): Also they're overdefended next turn if they want to block with another Colossus instead of Cauterizer.

[2018-04-09 19:47] apooche: Could be good or bad depending on the rest of the context
[2018-04-09 19:54] .bky_1556 (bky): Protip for thermite core abusers: If you have an odd amount of damage, you can click one pixie to make it even and activate the cores without wasting any damage.

messages.content: Don't freeze the botch, they soak with it and fire Cauterizer.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Freeze it and they soak with an engineer and the Cauterizer can't fire.

messages.author: .bky_1556
